behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is classifier-to-client handoff: taxonomy values can be correct in one module but dropped/mutated when building the outbound payload. No real OS/network needed, and correctness does not depend on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with a fake in-process API transport that captures outbound payloads; invoke classify with a known taxonomy and assert every valid label is present in the actual dispatched request body.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is local normalization/validation logic on returned label values. No real infrastructure or model behavior is required to reproduce the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the label-normalization/validation function (or smallest public method around it) with an out-of-taxonomy label fixture and assert output is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Determinism of normalization for identical inputs is a pure function property. Any higher-level test would be unnecessary for this specific failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Run normalization multiple times with the same raw-response fixture and assert the final label is identical across runs.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI API calls and consistency of model-produced labels. A stub would mask real model/provider variability, so Integration is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Execute repeated real provider calls with fixed document/taxonomy, record raw provider responses and final labels, and assert label stability when raw provider response is unchanged; treat changed provider responses as allowed variance.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Token-budget enforcement before dispatch is local request-construction policy logic. It can be validated without real HTTP/subprocess/LLM output.
behaviors.test_description: Given controlled input lengths and budget configs, assert constructor outputs the policy-correct result (truncate or reject) and that dispatch is not attempted when policy says reject.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires real provider token accounting on an AI API path. Mocked usage counters can always pass and hide real provider/model-side tokenization differences; Integration is disqualified by LLM/API dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run classification against the real provider with representative documents, read provider-reported token usage from responses/metadata, and assert usage does not exceed configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This tests retry/backoff orchestration around API errors, not model decision quality. An in-process stub that returns rate-limit then success can reproduce the failure without real network.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client scripted to return rate-limit error on first call and success on retry; assert module waits (backoff hook/timer observation), retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing initial error.
